sections.question:
In particular, do the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, including the Act's administrative remedies, apply to Assemblywoman Lee's participating in or attempting to influence decisions involving the University of California, the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, or any branches of the University of California?

The following specific questions arise:

A. Under analogous reasoning [to FPPC Informal Assistance Letter I-90-719], will Assemblywoman Lee not have conflicts of interest with respect to legislative decisions affecting the University of California, the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, and any of the branches of the University of California?

(1) Does legislation that has a material effect on the University of California, the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, or any of the branches of the University of California affect all residents of the state or a significant segment of the residents of the state and thus have a similar effect on the public generally or a significant segment of the public?

(2) Does legislation that has a material effect on the University of California, the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, or any of the branches of the University of California have a similar impact on "more than a small number of persons"?

(3) Is legislation that has a material effect on the University of California, the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, or any of the branches of the University of California of a general nature as defined by the California Constitution or otherwise not nongeneral legislation as defined by the Act?

B. If Assemblywoman Lee could have a conflict of interest with respect to some decisions affecting the University of California, the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, or one of the branches of the University of California, would Assemblywoman Lee have a conflict of interest with respect to: (1) AB 3320 - Regents of the University of California: meetings; (2) A bill that affected only the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory; (3) A bill that affected only one branch of the University of California.

C. Other than decisions discussed above, can you identify any types of decisions that would create a conflict of interest for Assemblywoman Lee?
sections.question_synthetic: Does Assemblywoman Barbara Lee, owner of a personnel consulting firm, have conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act's nongeneral legislation provisions when participating in decisions affecting the University of California, Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, or UC branches? Specifically, does legislation affecting these entities have a similar impact on the public generally or a significant segment of the public, and would specific bills create conflicts of interest?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion section present. Document contains detailed question section with multiple sub-questions (A, B, C) regarding applicability of nongeneral legislation conflict of interest rules. References prior FPPC letter I-90-719 regarding Assemblywoman Lee and Caltrans. OCR quality is generally good with minor scanning artifacts.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: In particular, do the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, including the Act's administrative remedies, apply to Assemblywoman Lee's participating in or attempting to influence decisions involving the University of California, the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, or any branches of the University of California?

The following specific questions arise:

A. Under analogous reasoning [to FPPC Informal Assistance Letter I-90-719], will Assemblywoman Lee not have conflicts of interest with respect to legislative decisions affecting the University of California, the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, and any of the branches of the University of California?

(1) Does legislation that has a material effect on the University of California, the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, or any of the branches of the University of California affect all residents of the state or a significant segment of the residents of the state and thus have a similar effect on the public generally or a significant segment of the public?

(2) Does legislation that has a material effect on the University of California, the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, or any of the branches of the University of California have a similar impact on "more than a small number of persons"?

(3) Is legislation that has a material effect on the University of California, the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, or any of the branches of the University of California of a general nature as defined by the California Constitution or otherwise not nongeneral legislation as defined by the Act?

B. If Assemblywoman Lee could have a conflict of interest with respect to some decisions affecting the University of California, the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, or one of the branches of the University of California, would Assemblywoman Lee have a conflict of interest with respect to: (1) AB 3320 - Regents of the University of California: meetings; (2) A bill that affected only the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory; (3) A bill that affected only one branch of the University of California.

C. Other than decisions discussed above, can you identify any types of decisions that would create a conflict of interest for Assemblywoman Lee?

embedding.summary: Attorney letter requesting FPPC assistance on behalf of Assemblywoman Barbara Lee regarding whether conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act apply to her legislative decisions involving the University of California and Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, given her ownership of a personnel consulting firm seeking UC contracts.
